data.title: Gold survives pre-weekend profit-taking to rack up seventh straight weekly gain
data.description:
Gold futures finished sharply lower in a bout of pre-holiday weekend profit-taking on Friday, but still closed out its seventh straight positive week, driven by fears of a global trade war in the wake of President Trump's continued push for tariffs.

Gold's bullish trend remains, Allegiance Gold's Alex Ebkarian asserted, driven by several factors including tariffs, underlying inflation, and a weaker U.S. dollar, with a growing shift from paper to physical gold further fueling the trend.

Meanwhile, speculation grew that the new reciprocal tariffs threatened by Trump are mainly intended as a negotiating tool after the president ordered agency heads to first study the tariffs on a country-by-country basis before potentially implementing them, a process that could take several weeks or months to complete.

President Trump's decision "eased initial market jitters and led to a weaker dollar, as fears of an escalating trade war subsided for now," Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for February ended +0.6% for the week to $2,883.60/oz for a seventh straight weekly gain, the longest winning streak since September-October 2024, and front-month February silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled +1.4% to $32.801/oz, the sixth weekly gain out of seven; on Friday, gold fell 1.4% while silver gained 0.4%.

Front-month Comex copper (HG1:COM) for February delivery closed +1.6% to $4.6565/lb this week, after falling 2.3% on Friday; Comex copper has jumped 19% YTD, compared with 9.5% on the London Metal Exchange.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ), (CPER), (COPX), (OTC:JJCTF)

Gold will outperform most commodities in 2025, as concerns around Trump's tariffs drive safe-haven demand, BMI analysts said.

Gold is benefiting from U.S. policy uncertainty, trade tensions, military conflicts, and general macroeconomic uncertainty, but the Federal Reserve likely will take a more cautious approach to interest rate cuts, particularly given the higher than expected January inflation print, which will cap price strength, BMI said.

data.title: AutoNation plans mixed shelf offering
data.description:
• AutoNation (NYSE:AN) may offer common stock, preferred stock, debt securities, warrants, subscription rights, depositary shares, purchase contracts, or units in future offerings.
  • Offering size not disclosed.
  • Source: SEC Filing

data.title: 1847 Holdings announces offering of up to 480.23M shares by selling shareholders
data.description:
• 1847 Holdings (NYSE:EFSH) announced offering of up to 480.23M common shares for sale by selling shareholders.
  • The shares include 3.44M issued shares, 38.87M shares from pre-funded warrants, 285.6M shares from Series A warrants, and 152.32M shares from Series B warrants.
  • The company will not receive proceeds from sales or exercise of pre-funded or Series A warrants but will receive funds from Series B warrant exercises.
  • Source: SEC Filing

data.title: Paulson & Co. adds Alphabet, exits Altice USA
data.description:
Paulson & Co. added Alphabet (GOOGL) in the latest quarter, according to the hedge fund's 13F filing.

The fund exited its stake in Altice (ATUS), Southwestern Energy (SWN) and Tellurian (TELL) according to the filing on Friday.

Paulson & Co. increased its positing in Thryv Holdings (THRY), adding 2.28M shares, according to the 13F.

data.title: Brand Engagement Network announces public offering of shares and warrants
data.description:
• Brand Engagement Network (NASDAQ:BNAI) announced a public offering of shares and warrants.
  • The offering includes pre-funded warrants.
  • Common warrants will have a five-year expiration from stockholder approval.
  • Source: SEC Filing

data.title: Dan Loeb's Third Point exits Bath & Body Works, enters Capital One among trades in Q4
data.description:
Third Point's Q4 2024 moves included exiting its stake in Bath & Body Works (NYSE:BBWI) after holding about 11.79M shares in the previous quarter, according to the hedge fund’s latest 13F filing.

The firm founded by Daniel Loeb also exited Apple (AAPL) by offloading 930K shares, according to its regulatory filing for the quarter ended December 31, 2024, published Friday. It left CVS (CVS) after holding ~1.57M shares in Q3, and stepped out of stakes in Amphenol (APH) and in Roper (ROP).

Third Point entered into stakes in financial companies Capital One (NYSE:COF) and Discover (DFS), purchasing 925K and 640K shares, respectively. It also picked up equity holdings in Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO), Fortive (FTV) and Workday (WDAY).

The hedge fund increased its stake in Tesla (TSLA), to 500K shares from 400K, and in Meta (META), to 665K from 545K. It also bolstered holdings in Telephone and Data Systems (TDS), LPL Financial (LPLA), and sports betting company Flutter Entertainment (FLUT).

It reduced stakes in Microsoft (MSFT), Cinemark (CNK), Vistra (VST), Danaher (DHR) and Apollo Global (APO).

data.title: Michael Burry's Scion adds Estee Lauder, exits Shift4, among Q4 trades
data.description:
Scion Asset Management, run by Michael Burry of "The Big Short" fame, took new stakes in Estee Lauder (NYSE:EL), PDD Holdings (NASDAQ:PDD), and HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA), and closed out positions in Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR), Olaplex Holdings (OLPX), and The RealReal (REAL) during Q4 2024, it disclosed in its 13F filing on Friday.

The firm added 100K shares of Estee Lauder (NYSE:EL) for a stake valued at ~$7.50M at Dec. 31, 2024; 75K shares in PDD (NASDAQ:PDD) with a value of $7.27M; and 15K shares in HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) with a value of $4.50M.

The fund increased its holdings in American Coastal (ACIC) to 146K shares (value ~$1.97M) from 100K shares.

It reduced its stakes in JD.com (JD) to 300K shares (value $10.4M) from 500K shares; Alibaba (BABA) to 150K shares (value $12.7M) from 200K; and Molina (MOH) to 25K shares (value ~$7.28M) from 30K shares, according to the filing.

data.title: Glenview Capital adds Microsoft, exits Marvell, Seagate, others
data.description:
Glenview Capital added Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Centene (CNC) with 1.29M shares, and Pinterest (PINS) in its latest quarter, according to a 13F filing.

The hedge fund exited stakes in Marvell (NASDAQ:MRVL), Seagate (STX), and Match Group in Q4, according to the filing on Friday.

Glenview boosted its stakes in ZoomInfo (ZI), Viatris (VTRS), and Alight (ALIT), adding 14M shares, and Myriad Genetics (MYGN) in Q4.

The fund decreased its stakes in Cigna (CI), Corteva (CTVA), and Clarivate (CLVT), shedding 7.7M shares, according to the 13F filing.

data.title: TechPrecision announce CEO as interim principal financial officer
data.description:
• TechPrecision (NASDAQ:TPCS) board appointed Alexander Shen, the chief executive officer, to serve as the interim principal financial officer and principal accounting officer of the company, effective February 14, 2025, after the previously disclosed resignation of Richard Roomberg, the company's former CFO.
  • The company is unable to file its quarterly report for the period ended December 31, 2024, within the prescribed time period.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Orvana Minerals announces director appointments
data.description:
• Orvana Minerals (TSX:ORV:CA) announced that Michael Davies, Alfredo Garcia Gonzalez, Robert Metcalfe and Michael Mutchler were elected as directors of the company. 
  • Robert Metcalfe was re-appointed as chairman of the board. 
  • The company will also re-appoint PricewaterhouseCoopers as the company's independent auditor for the ensuing year. 
  • Source: Press release
